goal: Improve leader prompt role hierarchy clarity. In internal/provider/protocol.go buildLeaderPrompt, update the opening instruction text to explicitly state: "You are a leader component operating under an orchestrator supervisor. The supervisor agent monitors your decisions via MCP tools and may inject [SUPERVISOR] directives into the leader context. You coordinate workers (executor, reviewer, tester) but do not perform implementation yourself." Also in buildWorkerPrompt, add: "You are an executor worker assigned by the leader. You perform the implementation task described below. Report results accurately including files changed, commands run, and any errors encountered." Also in buildPlannerPrompt and buildEvaluatorPrompt, add similar role-hierarchy descriptions. Run go build ./... and go test ./... to verify.

verification_contract.goal: Verify that role-hierarchy prompt text was updated correctly in `internal/provider/protocol.go` and that the repository still builds and tests cleanly.
scope: Prompt text changes for leader, worker, planner, and evaluator roles.
Build and test verification from the workspace root.
Documentation consistency only where directly impacted by the prompt text change.
required_checks: Confirm `buildLeaderPrompt` contains the explicit supervisor-monitoring and non-implementation wording.
Confirm `buildWorkerPrompt` contains executor assignment and reporting expectations.
Confirm `buildPlannerPrompt` and `buildEvaluatorPrompt` include analogous hierarchy descriptions.
Confirm build command succeeds without errors.
Confirm test command succeeds without failures.
disallowed_actions: Do not change orchestration logic beyond prompt text and directly related wording cleanup.
Do not bypass evaluator-gate, approval, or harness principles.
Do not introduce Windows-specific behavior into core logic.
Do not modify unrelated adapters, state-machine behavior, or roadmap features.
verification_contract.notes: Keep edits minimal and localized. If documentation does not explicitly cover these prompt introductions, no doc change is required beyond preserving consistency with repository standards.
